expectations.text: Asks at least 2 clarifying questions before proposing a solution
expectations.evidence: 5 questions (3 critical + 2 nice-to-have). Critical Q1 flags the fundamental mismatch: 'This project has no web application.' Q2 asks what the web app is for. Q3 asks who authenticates. Plus token storage and session duration.

expectations.text: References actual files or patterns from the cape project codebase
expectations.evidence: References .claude-plugin/plugin.json, hooks/hooks.json, settings.json. States: 'No package.json exists. No Node.js application code exists. No Express server, no routes, no middleware, no existing auth system.'

expectations.text: Proposes at least 2 distinct approaches with pros and cons
expectations.evidence: 3 approaches: (1) Express + passport.js + Google OAuth2 (recommended), (2) Hono + custom Google OAuth2 (alternative), (3) No web server — keep plugin-only (pushback). All have pros, cons, rationale.

expectations.text: Epic contains a Requirements (IMMUTABLE) section with specific, testable statements
expectations.evidence: Epic has '## Requirements (IMMUTABLE)' with 6 items: 'Admins authenticate via Google OAuth2 using passport.js', 'Tokens stored in httpOnly cookies (NOT localStorage)', 'Sessions expire after 24h of inactivity', etc.

expectations.text: Anti-patterns use 'NO X (reason: Y)' format with reasoning
expectations.evidence: 6 anti-patterns: 'NO localStorage tokens (reason: httpOnly prevents XSS token theft)', 'NO tokens in response body (reason: cookie-only transport prevents interception)', 'NO hardcoded credentials (reason: secrets must come from environment variables)', etc.

expectations.text: Creates exactly one first task, not zero or multiple
expectations.evidence: Exactly one task: 'Task 1: Scaffold Express server with passport.js Google OAuth2' with goal, TDD test cases, and 7-item implementation checklist.
No source code files created. Output is design documentation with architecture diagram and task definition.

expectations.text: Includes a Key Decisions table with Question/Answer/Implication columns
expectations.evidence: Key decisions table with 6 rows: Is this the right project, What is the web app for, Who authenticates, Token storage, Session duration, Credential management. All three columns present.
